description: Machine-readable catalog for the m-dev-tools GitHub organization. Summary-only: each tools.* entry carries identification + pointer URLs into the source repo's dist/repo.meta.json.exposes.* payloads. Intent routing lives in profile/task_index.json (Phase-1 Track B's build-catalog.py merges them at build time). No inlined facts (no subcommand lists, no module dictionaries, no rule arrays) — those drift; the pointer URLs don't.

org.name: m-dev-tools
org.tagline: Engine-neutral M (MUMPS) developer tools — TDD + CI/CD stack for InterSystems IRIS and YottaDB.
first_principles: m-stdlib has architectural priority over m-cli — when both projects need a utility, it lands in m-stdlib first.
Source-level tools (m fmt, m lint) are engine-neutral; runtime tools (m test, m coverage) are YottaDB-targeted.
Every module ships with a vendored conformance corpus tied to the relevant RFC or NIST publication.

m-cli's CLI ergonomics deliberately mirror Go (`go fmt`/`test`/`vet`) and Rust (`cargo fmt`/`test`/`clippy`) — see .github/docs/history/m-tool-gap-analysis.md for the gap analysis that drove the design.

m-cli.role: Canonical CLI toolchain — m fmt / lint / test / coverage / watch / lsp / doc / new / ...

m-cli-extras.role: Out-of-tree m-cli plugins — niche subcommands registered via the m_cli.plugins entry-point group

m-stdlib.role: Pure-M (and selectively $ZF-bound) runtime standard library — STD* modules

m-standard.role: Citable, machine-readable M language reference reconciling AnnoStd / YottaDB / IRIS / VA SAC

tree-sitter-m.role: Tree-sitter grammar for M — generated mechanically from m-standard's grammar-surface.json

m-test-engine.role: Minimal YottaDB Docker container — lightweight default test substrate for non-VistA M development

m-modern-corpus.role: Validation corpus — modern non-VistA M source from active open-source projects

tree-sitter-m-vscode.role: VS Code extension — syntax highlighting via tree-sitter-m WASM + LSP client (spawns `m lsp` from m-cli)

m-stdlib-vscode.role: VS Code extension — manifest-driven hover / goto-def / completion for STD* symbols in .m files

tdd_inner_loop.description: Canonical red → green → refactor loop using m-dev-tools. Mirrors `cargo new && cargo test && cargo clippy && cargo fmt` end to end.

steps.action: Create a new M project with Makefile, .m-cli.toml, tests/, CI
Write a failing test in tests/MYRTNTST.m using STDASSERT
Implement the routine until tests pass
Optional — leave watch running for sub-second feedback
steps.note: skip steps 4–6 manually after this; the watcher reruns affected suites on save

steps.action: Push — CI runs the same gates via `make ci`

minimal_application_recipe.description: Smallest realistic project: parse a JSON HTTP response and log it. Hits STDHTTP + STDJSON + STDLOG end-to-end.

steps.action: Write tests/FETCHTST.m with STDASSERT + STDMOCK (mock STDHTTP)
Implement src/fetch.m using STDHTTP for the call, STDJSON for parsing, STDLOG output

steps.command: m test && m fmt && m lint

discovery_protocol._comment: How an AI agent should consume this catalog when given a fresh user request.
step_1_read: llms.txt — human-sized AI entrypoint and priority reading order
discovery_protocol.step_2_match_intent: Map the user's plain-English request to one or more task_index categories[*].* keys. Prefer exact keys first, then fuzzy keyword match against each `intent` string.
discovery_protocol.step_3_resolve_tools: For each matched task, follow `primary` and `see_also` typed-IDs into the `tools` map. The kind prefix (`tool:` / `cmd:` / `module:` / `data:`) tells you what surface to expect. Follow the *_url pointers for machine-readable payloads.
discovery_protocol.step_4_load_local_context: Before changing a repo, read that repo's AGENTS.md (canonical) — repo-local instructions override org-level generalities.
discovery_protocol.step_5_load_manifests: For symbol-level work, fetch the relevant *_url payload. Use m-stdlib's manifest_url for STD* APIs; m-standard's *_url tables for language semantics; m-cli's commands_url / lint_rules_url / fmt_rules_url for tooling capabilities.
discovery_protocol.step_6_compose_workflow: Use `workflow.tdd_inner_loop` for new application work: scaffold, write failing test, implement, run test, fmt, lint, coverage, CI.
discovery_protocol.step_7_verify: Run the nearest available toolchain checks (`m test`, `m fmt --check`, `m lint`, `m coverage`, `m doctor`) before declaring completion.
discovery_protocol.step_8_do_not_invent: If a requested STD* API, m-cli command, lint rule, or language token is absent from the relevant *_url payload, do not fabricate it. State the gap and route the addition to the owning repo.
